reviews.review_msg: Very clear that Nilima is a master at numerical analysis. All lectures were written on a black board which is insane considering the material. Always answered emails on the day of sending, and was generally kind to students and super passionate about the course. Difficult homework, regularly took over 20 hours to complete and 50 percent was avg.
Prof Nilima is great!
Professor Nilima is very kind and keen on answering questions. Lectures are based on textbooks and exams are fair. Definitely recommend !

This prof made me want to come to lecture, she genuinely wants us to understand and do well. Other comments are right that lectures can be disorganized at times, but she's always willing to double back if anyone is confused. Proof assignments sometimes had confusing grading, attend class for her written examples!
Best math prof @ SFU. Incredible lecturer, well-crafted assignments, very fair exams. She set aside lots of time for office hours each week. Clearly cared about our learning.

So much textbook reading. Lectures essentially cover 300 pages of text. Lectures disorganized visually with so much fast writing. "Study everything" for tests. Mean in office hours; made me feel stupid for asking questions and discouraged me from attending. 5 hours of OH a week was nice. LONG assignments, but MATLAB is cool. NOT LIKE MACM316 AT ALL

Very helpful outside of class at office hours, but I found the lectures and notes themselves to be very poorly organized. We would often be given the "key idea" to understanding a particular topic, but the surrounding details felt glossed over, making the idea effectively useless to know. I found the midterms to be very fair. 5/5 person 1/5 lecture

This is a great class and Nilima Nigam is a great professor. She explained a lot of hard stuff in a way such that people can understand and I benefitted a lot from it. Wish she also showed class averages for exams so we wouldn't be in the dark about how we're doing.

Professor Nigam is by far one of my favourite professors I've had so far in my degree. She is definitely passionate about math and that carries over into her teaching. Most of the lectures are spent deriving formulas and going over theory/concepts but there are more examples in her posted notes. Midterm was very fair but marked somewhat harshly

I did well in this class but more so out of my love for deeply understanding math and self-cleaning skills NOT the professor. This professor assaults the mental health and confidence of perfectly good and very intelligent students. It is unnecessary and she plays cruel psychological games with students. It is shameful.

Macm 409 is a challenging and useful course. Course materials are all about numerical algorithms. Dr. Nigam shows us how those algorithms could be used in mathematical and data science field. There are lots of interesting applications in the homework. She is very helpful and friendly in her office hour. Besides, She has high expectation.

Doesn't say class is mandatory but gives specific info to only those in class so you must attend. Extremely unfair exams, quizzes are OK. Class averages were between 30-45%, very unfair. Causes way too much stress, plays with students minds, generally rude demeanour. I've heard she is better with upper level courses, but was horrible for 232.

(MACM409) I speak on behalf of 3 others along with myself in saying that I've never taken a more stressful and difficult class. If you have less than 1 year of computer science classes under your belt, you will probably get an F or C- Frequently assigns HW involving reading research papers and discussing unrelated ideas from that covered in class

If you do all the homework each week, you will get a nice grade. Her second midterm is hard but the final exam is very easy. If you look at the last few questions at each chapter, you will see the knowledge which the professor teach to us is limited and you will see how hard is linear algebra. Study by yourself!(make full use of your holiday)
This woman tends to throw off remark like "shame on you" to students, which gives a very clear picture of her unethical soul and uncaring character. She should be dismissed as soon as possible!

Linear algebra isn't a hard course but the way she teaches it makes it so. Sometimes she seems annoyed when you ask questions. The way she teaches is hard to follow and the material is partially covered, leaving you with a lack of knowledge for future courses. "If you fail or succeed it's all up to you, I can't help it" -N. Nigam during a lecture.

Nilima is just an overall awful prof. If this was a higher level course with a much higher learning curve I don't know what I would have done. Cons: Nilima is very bad at teaching and her lectures don't reflect the course material well. She also taught 4 sections on the last class which was during/right before finals. Stay AWAY
Poorly explained more difficult concepts. Lectures were just covering proofs theorems and basics, while there will little no applications to the problems that we saw on the exams.

Its 9:30 in the morning, two hours before the exam and I know that none of the exam will be based of practice or the concepts from class. It will be underhanded questions that relates to nothing that I learnt from her. Self-teaching is the key when you take a class with her. Warning, she is not the prof for math

Quizzes were based off homework which meant if you did the homework for the week you could easily get 100%. But midterms rolled around and you realized that the homework made no difference. It seems she was possibly behind as the last week she crammed several chapters into the last class and said it would be on the final. Seems nice though I guess

Linear algebra isn't to difficult, the problem is that what is taught in class and through the textbook has little relevance on the midterms and final. She just goes through a bunch of basic concept examples, then expects you to figure it out on the midterms. This is evident by the extremely low averages. Would stay clear if possible.
She just thinks outloud and does problems in front of you. Doesn't really know how to teach.
Bad at explaining concepts, midterms have nothing to do with lectures, assignments or quizzes

Dr. Nigam is by far the best math prof I've had during my undergraduate career. She truly cares about each student's learning, encourages discussion and participation in class and takes her time with you in her office hours to re-explain concepts you're struggling with. Warning: this class is rough and the homework is brutal. You need to work hard
Such a wonderful prof. Very difficult, but very caring for her students.

This is simultaneously one of the most stressful and rewarding courses I have ever taken. Dr. Nigam has the strange ability to really motivate her students to work hard, but damn do you have to work hard to achieve anything in this course. She starts the course off by basically talking you out of it, but gives A LOT of support throughout.
She's a terrible prof. Steer clear in any way you can. The average for midterm 2 was 40%.. she's also ruthless in lectures and she has a bad attitude towards her students.

Midterm gives wired questions and you will find them nowhere on your textbook nor assignments. You likely get no exact examples but more complicated proof after you are introduced to a new theory. The way she teaches is for 400 level MATH major students not students who are taking this course for requirement.

Worst teacher ! Midterm is only fousing in the concepts, not application. Many homework and midterm is never based on it . Worst ever! Its no teach students figure out questions. She wants everyone to fail this course. Worst ever

Class of 200+ people but she wont use a microphone. Honestly the worst teacher Ive had at sfu! Get ready to learn yourself if you have here because lectures will make you more confused. Goes way to fast explaining concepts

Horrible Professor, try and avoid if possible. Lectures are basically useless since she always wastes time going over a basic concept for 20+ minutes. Her weekly quizzes are organized terribly and her notes are impossible to follow.

I enjoyed her lectures. Assignments and exams are fair. However, I once waited for her office hour for an entire hour. She skipped it without any notice and would not make up for it. She also owed me money for some food expenses I claimed from a math competition. She claimed she could not find the receipts... Not a role model I can look up to...

Great Professor who cares about her students. Tests were very fare (midterm harder than final) with final being pretty much just direct applications of the concepts we learned throughout the semester.

Not very clear, does too much theory, and when she does do examples she skips a lot of steps, so you better have understood them the first time.  Tests were very hard, and she would not offer any alternate grading scheme so even if you work hard and learn the material by the end, it won't help you if you did bad on previous tests/homework.
Not very helpful. Steer clear if possible.
Her notes are excellent & very well organized, and she really cares for her students, though she is unsympathetic to a lack of mathematical maturity.  Excellent prof for senior math students.

Worst teacher I've ever had. Did several incomprehensible theorems and proofs every class and gave impossible homework. Gave a list of  70 theorems we had to be able to state and 30 theorems we had to be able to prove for the final. She barely asked anything from the list on the final exam. Inapproachable and an extremely incompetent teacher.

i don't know who made her a teacher. she doesn't teach, she just throws out an unlimited number of theorems/proofs in your face, and magically u re supposed to know everything in the textbook. worst proffessor ever, and worst class ever

Worst teacher ever. No ifs, ands, or buts. Not only is she useless at explaining anything, she also gives extremely tough and mostly irrelevant exams. To top it off, she's an absolute witch!! Under no circumstances should you take a course with her.
Very difficult. Doesn't help much.
